description: This set of SEM micrographs shows the cross-sectional microstructure of Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel after the 750 °C intermediate heat treatment step in the multi-stage heat treatment (MHT) process. Subplot (a) with a 10 μm scale bar presents the general microstructure, including austenite grains and clear grain boundary networks, with the dashed box marking the region magnified in subplot (b). Subplot (b) with a 2 μm scale bar shows the high-magnification morphology of the selected grain boundary region, where a large number of fine precipitates are observed distributing along grain boundaries. These closely spaced precipitates increase the stress in non-twinned regions, provide more twin nucleation sites, raise the proportion of special grain boundaries to interfere with random high-angle grain boundaries, and finally improve the impact toughness of the steel without sacrificing its high strength and ductility, supporting the core conclusion of MHT optimization for grain boundary structure.
